response: To cut out sugar, start by reading nutrition labels to identify hidden sugars in your favorite [brand name] products. Replace sugary snacks with whole fruits or nuts that you can find at your local [grocery store]. Gradually reduce the amount of sugar you add to your coffee or tea each morning. Drink water or unsweetened tea instead of sodas and fruit juices. Cook more meals at home using fresh ingredients to control what goes into your food. Keep a food diary to track your progress and identify triggers for sugar cravings. With consistent effort, your taste buds will adjust, making naturally sweet foods taste sweeter over time.
